holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Dominant in private banking index — 36%+ weight. Post-HDFC merger universal bank with home loan + retail bank + SME powerhouse. Predictable NIMs (~4%) and industry-lowest NPA (<1.4% GNPA) justify premium valuation.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: 2nd largest private bank weight. Best risk-reward among large banks — ROE 18%, growth 18%, P/B expanding. iMobile app success and Kuda (Africa) partnerships drive next growth chapter beyond domestic retail.

holdings.name: Kotak Mahindra Bank
holdings.description: 3rd largest private bank weight. Known as 'fortress balance sheet' — near-zero NPAs, massive capital cushion. Kotak 811 breakthrough in digital savings. Uday Kotak succession completed; Ashok Vaswani as MD rebuilding growth momentum.

holdings.name: Axis Bank
holdings.description: 4th. Turnaround complete — net NPA <0.5%, ROE recovering to 15%. Citibank India (cards, wealth, mortgages) acquisition added 3M+ premium customers. Burgundy private banking platform for HNI/UHNI growing fast.

holdings.name: IndusInd Bank
holdings.description: 5th. Commercial vehicle, gem & jewellery and consumer finance specialist. Strong in South India through BFIL microfinance network. Management focused on LDR (Loan-to-Deposit) normalisation and liability franchise strengthening.

holdings.name: IDFC First Bank
holdings.description: Youngest of the large private banks, transformed from an infrastructure NBFC. Retail liability (savings account) franchise building rapidly. V. Vaidyanathan's customer-first strategy driving low-cost CASA growth. EV financing a new vertical.
